articles.title: Jewellery and antiques roadshow coming to Whitworth
articles.excerpt: A jewellery and antiques roadshow will visit The Ashcroft in Whitworth on Wednesday 15 July, offering same-day cash for unwanted valuables.
articles.summary: We Buy Vintage will hold a roadshow at The Ashcroft on Market Street, Whitworth, from 10am until 3pm on Wednesday 15 July 2026.
articles.content_html:
A jewellery and antiques roadshow will visit Whitworth on Wednesday 15 July 2026.

We Buy Vintage will hold the event at The Ashcroft, Market Street, Whitworth, OL12 8DP, from 10am until 3pm.

Residents are invited to bring unwanted valuables, jewellery and antiques to the roadshow, where the company says items can be converted into same-day cash.

Event details

  • Date: Wednesday 15 July 2026
  • Time: 10am to 3pm
  • Location: The Ashcroft, Market Street, Whitworth, OL12 8DP
  • Organiser: We Buy Vintage

Anyone seeking further information can call 0800 669 6010 or visit the We Buy Vintage website.

Visitors should satisfy themselves that they understand any valuation or sale terms before agreeing to sell an item.
articles.image_alt: Poster for the We Buy Vintage jewellery and antiques roadshow in Whitworth
articles.image_caption: The roadshow will be held at The Ashcroft in Whitworth on Wednesday 15 July.
articles.event_location: The Ashcroft, Market Street, Whitworth, OL12 8DP

articles.title: Indoor 5 A Side Football Sessions Announced at Whitworth Swimming Baths
articles.excerpt: New Back Care Yoga sessions are set to begin at Whitworth Swimming Baths, aimed at improving posture and alleviating back pain for participants.
articles.content_html:
A series of Back Care Yoga sessions will commence at Whitworth Swimming Baths, designed to assist individuals in improving their posture and managing back pain. The initiative aims to provide a supportive environment for participants to engage in gentle yoga practices that focus on strengthening the back and enhancing flexibility.

The sessions will be led by qualified instructors who specialise in back care and yoga therapy. Each class will incorporate techniques that promote relaxation and mindfulness, alongside physical exercises tailored to address common back issues. Participants of all skill levels are welcome, making it an inclusive opportunity for those seeking relief from discomfort or wishing to enhance their overall well-being.

Classes are scheduled to run weekly, with the first session set to take place on . Attendees are encouraged to wear comfortable clothing and bring their own yoga mats, although mats will be available for those who do not have one. The sessions are expected to last approximately one hour, allowing ample time for instruction and practice.

Local health professionals have endorsed the programme, recognising the benefits of yoga in managing back pain and improving posture. They highlight that regular participation in such activities can lead to significant improvements in physical health and quality of life.

The Back Care Yoga sessions at Whitworth Swimming Baths represent a valuable addition to the community's health and wellness offerings. They provide an accessible way for residents to engage in physical activity while focusing on specific health concerns related to the back.

For more information about the sessions, including how to register, interested individuals can contact Whitworth Swimming Baths directly or visit their website. The initiative is part of a broader effort to promote health and fitness within the local community.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.
